title: Halloween
prose: Live bats stream through the Great Hall all evening, swooping low over the candles, and a thousand carved pumpkins grin down from every ledge, each one easily big enough to sit in. Dumbledore's start-of-feast announcement is, as ever, mostly nonsense words that somehow make everyone laugh anyway.

The food, if anything, outdoes the start-of-term feast — roast pumpkin everything, treacle tart again, a cauldron of what turns out to be actual, drinkable, bubbling black-currant juice going round on request.
It's the first night since you arrived that the castle feels less like somewhere you're visiting and more like somewhere you live.

choices.label: Stay until the very last candle gutters out.
Head up early — tomorrow's classes won't wait.
